dataset: Emergency Severity Index (ESI) v4 Triage Algorithm

pi_items.verified_value: AHRQ Publication No. 12-0014 is the Emergency Severity Index: A Triage Tool for Emergency Department Care, Version 4: Implementation Handbook, 2012 Edition
pi_items.source: AHRQ official publication verified via web search

pi_items.metadata_value: Four decision points: A (lifesaving intervention), B (high-risk), C (resources), D (vital signs)
pi_items.verified_value: ESI v4 uses exactly 4 decision points in sequence: A (immediate life-saving intervention), B (high-risk situation/unstable), C (resource needs count), D (vital signs in danger zone)
pi_items.source: AHRQ ESI v4 algorithm specification and multiple clinical sources

pi_items.verified_value: Level 1: Resuscitation - patients requiring immediate life-saving interventions (intubation, surgical airway, emergency medication, hemodynamic interventions, procedural sedation, electrical therapy)
pi_items.source: AHRQ ESI v4 algorithm and clinical sources

pi_items.verified_value: Level 2: Emergent - high-risk situations (chest pain, stroke, suicidal ideation), or patient confused/lethargic/disoriented, or in severe pain/distress
pi_items.source: AHRQ ESI v4 algorithm and clinical sources

pi_items.metadata_value: Level 3: Urgent (multiple resources, stable with abnormal vital signs)
pi_items.verified_value: Level 3: Urgent - stable patient requiring 2 or more resources, with normal vital signs
Level 4: Less Urgent - stable patient requiring exactly 1 resource (simple laceration, sprained ankle)
Level 5: Non-Urgent - stable patient requiring no resources (prescription refill, minor complaint)

pi_items.metadata_value: Danger zone vital signs: HR >100, RR >20, SaO2 <92%
pi_items.verified_value: ESI algorithm defines danger zone vital signs as: Heart Rate >100 beats/min, Respiratory Rate >20 breaths/min, Oxygen Saturation <92%
pi_items.source: AHRQ ESI v4 algorithm specification and clinical research studies

pi_items.metadata_value: Resources include: labs, ECG, X-rays, CT/MRI, IV fluids, IV meds, specialty consult
pi_items.verified_value: ESI resource classification includes: laboratory tests, EKG, X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound, IV fluids, IV/IM or nebulized medications, specialty consultation. Services NOT counted: H&P, oral medications, tetanus shot, refills
pi_items.source: AHRQ ESI v4 algorithm and clinical sources

pi_items.metadata_value: High-risk includes: chest pain, stroke symptoms, suicidal ideation
pi_items.verified_value: ESI algorithm specifies high-risk situations including: chest pain suspicious for acute coronary syndrome, stroke symptoms, suicidal ideation, and other presentations with risk of deterioration
pi_items.source: AHRQ ESI v4 algorithm and clinical sources

pi_items.metadata_value: ESI Level 1: Red, Level 2: Orange, Level 3: Yellow, Level 4: Green, Level 5: Blue
pi_items.verified_value: These specific RGB color assignments are NOT part of the official ESI v4 specification. ESI v4 does not specify colors for levels. These colors appear to be custom visualization choices for this task (similar to but distinct from Manchester Triage System colors)
pi_items.source: AHRQ ESI v4 official specification does not include color coding. Manchester Triage System uses similar color scheme but for a different triage algorithm

pi_items.metadata_value: Required keywords: lifesaving, immediate, intervention, high risk, confused, lethargic, distress, resources, danger zone, vitals, esi level 1-5
pi_items.verified_value: All required keywords are present in the ESI v4 algorithm text embedded in setup_task.sh and are clinically accurate terms from the official AHRQ algorithm
pi_items.source: AHRQ ESI v4 algorithm text in setup_task.sh

pi_items.verified_value: Task explicitly requires saving to ~/Desktop/esi_triage_tree.drawio per task description requirement 4
Task explicitly requires exporting Page 1 as PNG to ~/Desktop/esi_triage_tree.png per task description requirement 4
setup_task.sh creates the algorithm reference file at /home/ga/Desktop/esi_v4_algorithm.txt with embedded ESI v4 text
Task description requires: Page 1 (main decision tree flow) and Page 2 (Legend/Definitions page)
Task requires at least 4 decision nodes (diamonds) plus 5 outcome nodes (rounded rectangles) = minimum 9 shapes
Task requires color-coding 5 ESI outcome nodes distinctively (Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue), exceeding minimum of 3 colors

privileged_info_summary: The ESI v4 algorithm is the genuine Emergency Severity Index Version 4 from AHRQ Publication No. 12-0014 (2012 Edition). The task correctly implements the core algorithm: Decision Point A asks about immediate lifesaving interventions (assigns Level 1: Resuscitation), Decision Point B identifies high-risk situations (assigns Level 2: Emergent), Decision Point C counts resources with thresholds (0→Level 5, 1→Level 4, ≥2→proceed), and Decision Point D checks vital signs (HR>100, RR>20, SaO2<92% can upgrade to Level 2). The five ESI levels are correctly named: Level 1 (Resuscitation), Level 2 (Emergent), Level 3 (Urgent), Level 4 (Less Urgent), Level 5 (Non-Urgent). Resource examples (labs, ECG, X-rays, CT/MRI, IV fluids, IV meds, specialty consult) and high-risk situations (chest pain, stroke, suicidal ideation) match AHRQ specifications. All required keywords are clinically accurate. The task's color assignments (Red→L1, Orange→L2, Yellow→L3, Green→L4, Blue→L5) are custom visualization choices not part of the official ESI specification.
